meta.feed_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

items.title: Israeli police destroy children’s footballs at Al-Aqsa mosque
items.summary: Israeli police confiscated and destroyed footballs that were being played with by children around Al-Aqsa mosque.

items.title: Trump says US will ‘start dropping bombs again’ if no Iran deal is reached
items.summary: US President Donald Trump says the US will “have to start dropping bombs again” if no US-Iran deal is reached.

items.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 50 of the US-Iran conflict?
items.summary: Iran reopens the Strait of Hormuz with conditions and threatens closure if the US blockade of Iranian ports continues.

items.title: Curry, Warriors knocked out of NBA play-in tournament by Suns
items.summary: Stephen Curry struggled with just 17 points as the 2026 NBA champions season was ended by the Phoenix Suns in play-in.

items.title: Al-Noor centre: A lifeline for blind children in Gaza
items.summary: Al-Noor is a UNRWA centre in Gaza providing education and support for visually impaired children.

items.title: New Jersey announces $150 transit tickets to reach the World Cup
items.summary: Transit fares from New York to MetLife Stadium in New Jersey for World Cup matches will rise to $150.

items.title: Lebanese man removes Israeli flag from castle in southern Lebanon
items.summary: A Lebanese man who returned to southern Lebanon removes the Israeli flag from Beaufort Castle (Qalaat al-Shaqif).

items.title: Trump thanks Gulf states for their ‘tremendous’ support
items.summary: ‘This will be a great and brilliant day for the world’ Donald Trump said as Iran fully opens the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: UN aid chief warns of possible ‘full-scale famine’ in South Sudan
items.summary: UN aid chief Tom Fletcher says that South Sudan is at risk of slipping into 'full-scale famine and collapse’.

items.title: Iran war live: Tehran says US blockade of ports must end as strait opens
items.summary: Tens of thousands of people forcibly displaced by Israeli attacks on Lebanon use first day of ceasefire to return home.

items.title: Pressure mounts on Peru’s election authorities amid presidential race delay
items.summary: The vote count continues to determine who will join conservative Keiko Fujimori in Peru's presidential run-off in June.

items.title: US judge blocks Justice Department bid to seize voter data in Rhode Island
items.summary: Ruling is latest loss for Trump administration, which has sought access to state voter data ahead of the US midterms.

items.title: Trump seeks ‘resolution’ of his $10bn lawsuit against IRS, spurring concern
items.summary: Critics warn that Trump could arrange his own settlement by negotiating with a US Justice Department under his control.

items.title: Iran rejects Trump claim on deal to surrender nuclear material stockpiles
items.summary: Iran has rejected suggestions by Donald Trump that it has agreed to hand over its stockpile of enriched uranium.

items.title: Lebanon president says country is no longer a pawn amid Israel ceasefire
items.summary: President Joseph Aoun promised to work to preserve Lebanon's sovereignty and freedom.

items.title: Starmer rejects calls to quit as pressure mounts over Mandelson vetting
items.summary: Top Foreign Office official takes fall for fiasco and resigns; Starmer promises to deliver 'relevant facts' on Monday.

items.title: Will the ceasefire in Lebanon hold?
items.summary: Lebanon and Israel are holding first direct talks in decades.

items.title: Displaced family reunited with grandfather in Lebanon’s Tyre
items.summary: A grandfather was reunited with his son and grandchildren in Tyre, southern Lebanon.

items.title: World Cup 2026: FIFA chief Infantino defends pricing and tickets in US
items.summary: Gianni Infantino responds to criticism for pricing of 2026 edition highlighting 'very special' US market as factor.

items.title: Why Marie-Louise Eta’s appointment should be the norm, not the exception
items.summary: As the German coach makes history, experts believe her appointment should not be an exception in men’s football.

items.title: UN warns South Sudan at risk of ‘full-scale famine’ as fighting intensifies
items.summary: UN aid chief Tom Fletcher says hunger is tightening its grip amid rising humanitarian needs and intensifying fighting.

items.title: US Congress extends controversial surveillance power under FISA for 10 days
items.summary: The measure has long been criticised for allowing US intelligence agencies to collect citizen data without a warrant.

items.title: World reacts to Strait of Hormuz reopening amid US-Iran conflict
items.summary: Politicians and industry welcome reopening of the vital waterway after throttling of traffic caused oil prices to spike.

items.title: US confirms transit fare spike to $150 for World Cup fans in New Jersey
items.summary: Fares confirmed at nearly 12-time hike to get from Manhattan’s Penn Station to the Metlife Stadium in East Rutherford.

items.title: Scholar Rumeysa Ozturk returns to Turkiye following Trump deportation push
items.summary: Ozturk says she made the decision to avoid 'losing more time to the state-imposed violence and hostility' under Trump.
